name: LINE-AD emtricitabine Alzheimer trial is recruiting without posted results
summary: LINE-AD is a direct placebo-controlled Alzheimer reverse-transcriptase-inhibitor trial-watch record, but it has no efficacy, safety, or target-engagement results yet.
statement: ClinicalTrials.gov lists NCT04500847 as a recruiting phase 1 randomized placebo-controlled emtricitabine study in 35 estimated MCI or Alzheimer disease participants, with safety, inflammatory biomarker, cognitive, functional, and CSF biomarker endpoints and no posted results as of June 6, 2026.
population_or_model: Adults and older adults aged 50 to 85 years with biomarker-confirmed MCI or mild to moderate Alzheimer disease
time_horizon: Six months of dosing with follow-up through about 7 to 8 months
quantitative_note: Estimated enrollment is 35 participants, allocated 25 active emtricitabine and 10 placebo in the registry arms.
caveats: Recruiting status and trial design do not provide evidence of benefit.
The registry's estimated primary completion date has passed while the record still lists recruiting status, so it needs surveillance for status and results changes.
The trial is Alzheimer disease and MCI context, not a general aging-prevention population.
Direct retrotransposon target-engagement endpoints are not clearly listed in the registry.
